title: Free Things to Do With Kids in Nelson
meta_desc: The best free and cheap things to do with kids in Nelson — Tahunanui Beach, the Centre of NZ walk, Saturday market and parks. Family-tested favourites on a budget.
intro: Sunny Nelson is a brilliant family base at the top of the South Island, and so much of its appeal is free. Tahunanui is one of the country's best family beaches, the short Centre of New Zealand walk rewards you with a panoramic view, and the famous Saturday market is free to browse and full of character. Here are our favourite free and cheap things to do with kids in Nelson.

sections.heading: Beaches & Tahunanui
sections.body: Tahunanui Beach is the family favourite — a long, flat, safe stretch of golden sand with shallow water, a free playground, a paddling pool, and even a small free hydroslide in summer at the modelling-pond reserve. There's masses of grass for picnics and run-around, making it an easy full day out for almost nothing. On a calm day the shallow lagoon area is perfect for toddlers.
list: Tahunanui Beach — free, flat golden sand, safe shallow water
Tahunanui playground & paddling pool — free, with summer water play
Beach reserve picnic lawns — free, room to run
Rabbit Island (Rabbit Island/Moturoa) — free beaches and forest tracks nearby

sections.heading: Walks & Lookouts
sections.body: The Centre of New Zealand walk is a Nelson must-do and it's free — a short but steep climb (about 20–30 minutes up) from the Botanical Reserve to a lookout marking the surveyed centre of the country, with a great view over the city and bay. The reserve at the bottom has open space for kids, and there are gentler riverside and city walks if little legs aren't up for the hill.
list: Centre of NZ walk — free, short climb, panoramic views
Botanical Reserve — free green space at the base of the walk
Maitai River walkway — free, flat riverside path with swimming holes
Grampians and Tantragee tracks — free hill walks near town

sections.heading: Gardens, Markets & Parks
sections.body: The Nelson Saturday Market in Montgomery Square is free to wander and a great morning out — buskers, food stalls and crafts. Isel Park is a free, gorgeous spot with huge old trees, open lawns and Broadgreen Historic House nearby, while the Miyazu Japanese Garden and Queens Gardens are both free, peaceful and pretty. The kids will happily lose an hour feeding ducks and crossing the little bridges.
list: Nelson Saturday Market — free to browse, buskers and food
Isel Park — free, big trees, lawns and a heritage house
Miyazu Japanese Garden — free, ponds and bridges
Queens Gardens — free Victorian gardens in the city centre

sections.heading: Cheap Treats & Rainy-Day Options
sections.body: Natureland Zoo at Tahunanui is a small, charming zoo with a modest entry fee (under-2s free) — a lovely cheap outing with meerkats, monkeys and farm animals. The Nelson Provincial Museum has free general admission for residents and cheap entry otherwise, making it a good wet-weather stop. The Riverside and central pools are cheap warm swims when the weather turns.
list: Natureland Zoo — small entry, meerkats and farm animals
Nelson Provincial Museum — free/cheap, rainy-day exhibits
Riverside Pool — cheap warm indoor swim
Nelson library — free play space and holiday programmes

sections.heading: Budget Summary
sections.body: With a top-tier free beach, a quick rewarding hill walk and free gardens and markets, Nelson is one of the easiest places to enjoy with kids on a budget. Here's how a free day adds up for a family of four.
list: Tahunanui Beach & playground: FREE
Centre of NZ walk: FREE
Isel Park, Miyazu & Queens Gardens: FREE
Nelson Saturday Market: FREE to browse
Pack-your-own picnic: ~$20 for a family of four
Estimated full-day cost: $0–$25
